D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Chromesthesia — Sound-to-Colour Mapping

Scriabin's "Clavier à lumières" gave each note a colour; modern chromesthetes report similar pitch-hue maps (often high pitch = bright, low pitch = dark)

Chromesthesia (~0.2% prevalence) maps pitch + timbre to colour. Scriabin used C=red, D=yellow, E=sky blue in Prometheus (1910). Music-colour mappings often correlate with letter-name (C = first note = red, like A = first letter = red).

Slide pitch on chromatic scale C–B.
